document: Introduction to Internet of Things Assignment-Week 1 Solutions

questions.questionText: What is the full form of IoT?
questions.explanation: The full form of IoT is "Internet of Things".

questions.questionText: The "Source Address" filed in the IPv6 header is of
questions.explanation: The IPv6 header uses 128 bits to represent the "Source Address". See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part II) @ 18:03

questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are not an enabler of IoT?
State true or false. The gateway is tasked with assigning a unique address to each of the field devices or sensors within its jurisdiction.
State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: The increasing number of devices in loT is expected to result in an address crunch.
questions.explanation: The increasing number of devices in IoT is expected to result in an address crunch. See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part II) @ 01:19

questions.questionText: In a node/network is connected to multiple networks for improved reliability.
questions.explanation: In multi-homing, a node/network is connected to multiple networks for improved reliability. See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part II) @ 15:22

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT a function of an IoT Gateway?
options: a. Connects the loT LAN to a WAN
b. Can implement several LAN and WAN
questions.explanation: The functionalities of an IoT Gateway are: a. Connects the IoT LAN to a WAN, b. Can implement several LAN and WAN, c. Forwards packets between LAN and WAN on the IP layer. See lecture 2 @ 3:09

questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are incorrect? Statement I: An actuator is a component of a machine or system that moves or controls the mechanism or the system. Statement II: An actuator requires a control signal and a source of energy.
Which of the following is NOT a function of an IoT LAN?
questions.explanation: The functionalities of an IoT Gateway are a. Local, short-range communication, b. Spreads across buildings or organization. See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 3:09

questions.questionText: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: The word "Transducer" is a collective term used for both Sensors and Actuators.
questions.explanation: The word "Transducer" is the collective term used for both Sensors, which can be used to sense a wide range of different energy forms such as movement, electrical signals, radiant energy, thermal, or magnetic energy, etc., and Actuators, which can be used to switch voltages or currents. See lecture 3 (Sensing) @ 11:39

questions.questionText: State True or False. Analog Sensors produce a discontinuous output signal or voltage, which is generally proportional to the quantity being measured.
questions.explanation: Analog Sensors produce a discontinuous output signal or voltage, which is generally proportional to the quantity being measured. See lecture 3 (Sensing) @ 15:24

questions.questionText: If the output signal differs from the correct value by a constant, the sensor has an
questions.explanation: If the output signal differs from the correct value by a constant, the sensor has an offset error or bias. See lecture 3 (Sensing) @ 20:33

questions.questionText: State True or False Pneumatic actuators enable the production of large forces from a relatively larger pressure changes.
questions.explanation: Pneumatic actuators enable the production of large forces from a relatively larger pressure changes. See lecture 4 (Actuation) @ 09:04

questions.questionText: A sensor node is made up of which of the following?
questions.explanation: A sensor node is made up of a combination of sensor/sensors, a processor unit, a radio unit, and a power unit. See Page number 101, Chapter 5, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.questionText: What is the full form of SMP in the context of IoT actuators?
questions.explanation: The full form of SMP is Shape Memory Polymers. See Page number-111, Chapter 5, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)
